merger_name: Autopact - Freehold interest in commercial land in Ferntree Gully, VIC

other_parties.name: FERNTREE GULLY AUTO SALES PTY LTD

merger_description:
Autopact Property No. 4 Pty Ltd (the **Acquirer**), a wholly owned subsidiary of Autopact Holdings Pty Ltd (together, **Autopact**), proposes to acquire the 100% freehold interest in the commercial land and buildings at 871–875 Burwood Highway, Ferntree Gully VIC 3156 (the **Property**) from Doidge Property Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**), by way of a contract of sale of land.

Autopact operates motor-vehicle dealerships across Queensland, New South Wales and Victoria, supplying new and used vehicles, finance and insurance, and servicing and parts.

The Vendor is a passive property-holding entity that does not itself supply vehicles, finance, servicing or parts, and does not operate any motor-vehicle dealership.

The Property is currently leased to Ferntree Gully Auto Sales Pty Ltd, another wholly owned subsidiary of Autopact Holdings Pty Ltd and connected entity of the Acquirer, which operates a motor-vehicle dealership at the Property. The lease terms extend to 2044.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Autopact Property No. 4 Pty Ltd and Autopact Holdings Pty Ltd
applied for a notification waiver in respect of Autopact Property No.
4 Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of 100% freehold interest in the
commercial land and buildings at 871–875 Burwood Highway,
Ferntree Gully VIC 3156 (the Property) from Doidge Property Pty Ltd
(the Vendor), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Autopact Property No. 4 Pty Ltd, is a wholly owned
subsidiary of Autopact Holdings Pty Ltd (together, Autopact).
Autopact operates motor-vehicle dealerships across Queensland,
New South Wales and Victoria, supplying new and used vehicles,
finance and insurance, and servicing and parts.
The Property is currently leased to Ferntree Gully Auto Sales Pty Ltd,
another wholly owned subsidiary of Autopact Holdings Pty Ltd and
connected entity of the acquirer, which operates a motor-vehicle
dealership at the Property. The lease terms extend to 2044.
The Vendor is a passive property-holding entity that does not itself
supply vehicles, finance, servicing or parts, and does not operate any
motor-vehicle dealership.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening
of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal competitive overlap
b. Autopact has been using the land as a lessee and intends to
continue using the land for the same purposes, but as an
owner
c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
